pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - The Washington Post's Climate Solutions initiative

pageCaption.value: The Washington Post’s ongoing “Climate Solutions” initiative launched in partnership with Rolex in 2018. For this series, The Post has focused on the individuals, companies and other organizations that are exploring ways to address our most significant environmental problems. The features below live alongside The Washington Post’s reporting and spotlight individual innovators at the forefront of finding climate solutions.

metadescription.value: The Washington Post has partnered with Rolex to put those at the forefront of finding climate solutions in the spotlight. Learn more on rolex.org.

text.value:
The Washington Post’s ongoing “Climate Solutions” initiative launched in partnership with Rolex in 2018. For this series, The Post has focused on the individuals, companies and other organizations that are exploring ways to address our most significant environmental problems.

The features below live alongside The Washington Post’s reporting and spotlight individual innovators at the forefront of finding climate solutions.

pageCaption.value: Adventuring is no longer just about pushing the boundaries of human achievement. Over the past century, pioneers have begun blending a passion for exploring the natural world with enduring commitment to protecting it.

WaPageTitle.value: How explorers helped seed planet conservation

metadescription.value: Polar explorer and climate change researcher, Alain Hubert is the perfect example of a modern adventurer. Find out what this entails on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: How explorers helped seed planet conservation

pageCaption.value: Inside the extraordinary effort of two environmentalists to safeguard the country’s lakes and rivers—and the life that depends on them.

WaPageTitle.value: Finding solutions to India’s freshwater challenge

metadescription.value: Two groundbreaking conservationists are helping to keep India’s freshwater systems – and the life that depends on them – protected. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Finding solutions to India’s freshwater challenge

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - An Explorer with a cause

pageCaption.value: For decades, Arctic adventurer Lonnie Dupre has been travelling to some of the world’s most extreme environments to raise awareness about the consequences of global warming.

metadescription.value: Lonnie Dupre travels to the world’s most extreme environments to raise awareness about the consequences of global warming. Find out more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Saving the world’s largest fish

pageCaption.value: How one leading conservationist is using crowd-sourced photography and an innovative software algorithm to help save the world’s largest fish

metadescription.value: Brad Norman is enlisting the help of people around the world to compile a database in an effort to save whale sharks. Discover more on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Vreni Häussermann has made it her mission to catalogue and conserve the extraordinary wildlife found in Chile’s deep fjords.

metadescription.value: Rolex Laureate and marine biologist, Vreni Häussermann is helping to preserve the marine biodiversity of Chilean Patagonia. Read more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - A better understanding of oceans

pageCaption.value: Two groundbreaking researchers are using new technology to better understand the seas, and animals that live in them.

WaPageTitle.value: Innovating for a better understanding of oceans

metadescription.value: Rolex Laureates Barbara Block and Michel André are using new technology to better understand the seas. Learn about their projects on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Innovating for a better understanding of oceans

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Finding novel ways to safeguard wildlife

pageCaption.value: How two environmental leaders are using innovative waste reuse strategies to spur economic development—and clean up their communities in the process.

metadescription.value: Rolex partners with scientists fighting to reduce the impact of human settlements and industries on wild landscapes. Discover more rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: How two environmental leaders are using innovative waste reuse strategies to spur economic development—and clean up their communities in the process.

metadescription.value: Rolex supports Reese Fernandez-Ruiz and David Schweidenback's goal to stem the accumulation of garbage around the world. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Campos-Silva's quest to save arapaimas

pageCaption.value: For biologist João Campos-Silva, protecting a single fish species is the key to improving the quality of countless human lives

WaPageTitle.value: Saving the giant of the Amazon

metadescription.value: Rolex partners with João Campos-Silva to show how we can combine biodiversity conservation together with people's wellbeing. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Saving the giant of the Amazon

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Topher White challenges deforestation

pageCaption.value: In rainforests around the world, conservationists are using an unexpected tool—mobile phones—to face the challenge of deforestation.

metadescription.value: Rolex supports White's innovative conservation method using recycled phones and a unique software to protect the rainforest. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - Reviving the world's coral reefs

pageCaption.value: Inside marine biologist Emma Camp's innovative plan to address coral bleaching.

metadescription.value: Marine biologist and Rolex Award Laureate Emma Camp has an innovative plan to bring living colour back to coral reefs. Discover more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Partnerships - How to solve the plastic problem

pageCaption.value: Can a new chemical innovation finally offer a solution for the world’s unrecyclable waste?

WaPageTitle.value: How to solve the plastic problem

metadescription.value: Eco-conscious from a young age, Miranda Wang is dedicated to solving the global plastic waste problem with Rolex's support. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: How to solve the plastic problem

title.value: The mighty Zambezi River and its secrets
The 2019 Rolex National Geographic Explorers of the Year - The Okavango Wilderness Project Team
Fernando Trujillo: For the love of river dolphins
